title: Tokenization's Next Use Case Is Personalized Portfolios, NYLIM Executive Says
summary: Thomas Sy, head of multi-asset solutions at the $800-million asset manager NYLIM, says blockchain can enable complex portfolio construction that's not yet possible in traditional finance.
body:
Thomas Sy, head of multi-asset solutions at NYLIM, an $800-million asset manager, believes blockchain tokenization is poised to transform personalized investment portfolios. He argues that the technology can enable complex portfolio construction that's currently not possible in traditional finance. This could democratize access to sophisticated investment strategies.

Sy's comments come as tokenization gains traction in the financial world. Tokenization involves converting assets into digital tokens on a blockchain, making them easier to trade and manage. This technology can create highly customized portfolios tailored to individual investor needs, something that's difficult to achieve with traditional financial instruments.

For everyday investors, this could mean access to more personalized and flexible investment options. Instead of relying on standardized products, investors could have portfolios designed specifically for their risk tolerance, financial goals, and preferences. This could make investing more efficient and potentially more profitable for a broader range of people.

NYLIM is not alone in exploring this potential. Other financial institutions are also looking into how tokenization can enhance their offerings. As the technology matures, we can expect to see more innovative investment products becoming available. Investors should watch for announcements from major asset managers about new tokenized portfolio options.
